lines.en_lit: MISHNA: They brought out the spoon and the coal pan He scoops his handfuls and places it into the spoon. The large corresponding to the large and the small corresponding to the small And this was the measure He took the coal pan in his right and the spoon in his left

GEMARA: taught coal pan: He takes a coal pan and ascends to the top of the altar and rakes and descends. There coal pan of coals, and here coal pan of incense, As it was taught They brought out an empty spoon for him from the chamber of vessels, and a coal pan heaped with incense from the Chamber of the House of Avtinas.

He scoops his handfuls and places it into the spoon. The large corresponding to the large and small corresponding to the small and this was the measure Why do I a spoon on Yom Kippur? the Merciful One states: his hands full and bring
because it is impossible As what should we do? Let him bring in and then bring in The Merciful One states one bringing and not two bringing.

let him take incense by his handfuls and place coal pan on top of and enter, when he arrives there, what should he do? Let him take coal pan in his teeth and lower Now, before a king of flesh and blood one would not do so, All the more so, before the King of kings, the Holy One, Blessed be He.
Therefore, it is impossible and since it is impossible he acts as we find princes
took the coal pan in his right and the spoon in his left The native is on the ground and the stranger is in the heavens;
this greater and that less. And even when the two of them are equal, like the action of Rabbi Yishmael ben Kimḥit, this is hot and that is cold

They said about Rabbi Yishmael ben Kimḥit that he would scoop four kav, by his handfuls, and say: All the women selected but the selection of my mother rose to the roof, Some say of flour, in accordance with Rabba bar Yonatan. As Rabba bar Yonatan said Rabbi Yeḥiel said: Flour is beneficial for the sick.

And some say in accordance with Rabbi Abbahu, as Rabbi Abbahu raised a contradiction It is written: “For You have girded me vatazreni with strength for battle” and it is written “Who girds me hame’azreni] with strength” David said before the Holy One, Blessed be He: Master of the Universe: You selected me [zeritani] You fashioned me [zeraztani]

They said about Rabbi Yishmael ben Kimḥit: Once he was talking to a certain Arab in the market and a drop sprayed from mouth onto the clothes And his brother Yeshevav entered and served in his stead. And, their mother saw two High Priests on a single day.

And they further said about Rabbi Yishmael ben Kimḥit: Once he went out and talked to a certain officer [hegmon] in the market, and a drop of saliva sprayed from mouth onto the clothes and his brother Yosef entered and served in his stead. And, their mother saw two High Priests on a single day.

The Sages taught Kimḥit had seven sons, and they all served of the High Priesthood, The Sages said to her: What did you perform to merit this? She said to them: In all my days, the beams of my house never saw the braids of my hair, They said to her: Many did so and did not succeed
The Sages taught: his handful, that he should not measure an amount for the handful A dilemma was raised before What is measure an amount for the handful

there, it as it is written “in his handful,” However here, where it is not written: In his hands, but “and his hands full of sweet incense beaten small” not Or perhaps is derived “full” “full,” from “his handful,”
Come hear And this was the measure What, is it not that if he wanted to measure a different amount, he measure No, this is said; And so too, would return and scoop inside
learn from this scoops and again scoops.
perhaps that if he wanted to measure a different amount, he measure Alternatively, that take neither less nor more,

The Sages taught: “His handfuls” might be overflowing the verse states: “His handful” If “his handful,” might even with his fingertips, the verse states: “His handfuls” as people take a handful, How He scoops his three fingers over the palm of his hand, and takes a handful
